revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:平尾山古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Hirano-Ogata Kofun Group 11-4, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Hirano-Ōgata No. 11 Group, Tomb No. 4 | alternate_name = Hirao Mountain Sen-tsuka Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|45.5|N|135|38|04.9|E}} | type = {{ill|Cluster Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cluster Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609514}} | period = Early 6th century to late 7th century | map_size = 8 clusters, 1,407 tumuli | historic_site = None | burial goods = | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Hirao
Mountain Kofun Cluster|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=45.5|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=04.9|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|45.5|N|135|38|04.9|E}}}}}} '''Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster''' (平尾山古墳群, '''Hirao Mountain Sen-tsuka Kofun Cluster''') is a {{ill|Cluster Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cluster Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609514}} located in the Takai and Ando areas of {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. == Overview == The cluster is situated on the southern hills of the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574917}}, comprising 1,407 kofun identified to date{{Cite web|author=Kashiwara City Board of Education, designation1 Division|url=http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016110100026/|title=7-1. Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|work=Kashiwara City|date=2017-03-24|accessdate=2020-09-29}}. The distribution spans an area of 3 km east to west and 2 km north to south. It includes eight subgroups: Ando, Takai, Hirao Mountain, Karitaobata, Honda, Taihei-ji, Hirano-Ōgata, and Aodani. While these are collectively termed as a single kofun cluster, there is some debate regarding this classification. The term often refers specifically to the areas with the highest concentration of tumuli, such as the Hirao Mountain and Karitaobata subgroups. The majority of tumuli are circular, with a diameter of around 10 meters. Excavations reveal that many feature {{ill|Horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, often small in map_size. A few examples of {{ill|Cut-stone chambers|ja|切石積|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cut-stone chambers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 切石積み|1=Ashlar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1122603}} and {{ill|Side-entrance chamber|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|lt=Side-entrance chambers|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} have also been identified. == built of Formation == The kofun cluster was constructed between the early 6th and late 7th centuries, with its peak in the late 6th to early 7th centuries. The Hirao Mountain and Karitaobata subgroups exhibit significant activity into the early 7th century, distinguishing this cluster from other contemporary examples. Alongside the {{ill|Takayasu Senzuka Kofun|en|Takayasu Senzuka Kofun|qid=Q11669134|lt=Takayasu Kofun Cluster|1=Takayasu Senzuka Kofun}} and {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|en|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11353283|lt=Ichisuga Kofun Cluster|1=Ichisuka Kofun Cluster}}, the Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster is one of the representative large-map_size kofun clusters of the {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. == built Groups == According to {{ill|Shiraishi Taichiro|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Taichiro|qq=}}, the Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster (Hirao Mountain Sen-tsuka Kofun Cluster) shares similarities with other kofun clusters in Kawachi, such as the Takayasu Kofun Cluster, through burial customs like miniature cooking implements included as grave goods. These suggest that the kofun were constructed by immigrant clans who settled in Kawachi. However, the Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster began construction in the early 6th century, peaking in the early 7th century, whereas the Takayasu Kofun Cluster concluded its construction by the early 7th century. Shiraishi hypothesizes that the Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster may have been associated with immigrant groups under the patronage of the {{ill|Soga clan|en|Soga clan|qid=Q909854|lt=Soga clan|1=Soga clan}}, while the Takayasu Kofun Cluster was associated with immigrant groups under the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, who were defeated in conflicts with the Soga clan at the end of the 6th century{{Sfn|Shiraishi|2009|11=290-298}}. == Gallery == Ando Kofun Group 6-3, sekishitsu.jpg|Ando Group, Tomb No. 6-3, reconstructed stone chamber Hirano-Ōgata Group Tomb 20-3 Monodragon.jpg|Hirano-Ōgata No. 20 Group, Tomb 3: Single Dragon-Headed Sword Pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Kashiwara Municipal History Museum.}} House-shaped Sarcophagus from Hirao Mountain Kofun Group.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus from Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster
{{small|Exhibited at {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|qid=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}}.}} == notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|author=Tetsu Takashima|chapter=Hirao Mountain Sen-tsuka Kofun Cluster|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shu11an|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Shu11an|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|year=1989|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|author={{ill|Shiraishi Taichiro|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Taichiro|qq=}}|title=Archaeological Perspectives on the Wa Kingdom|publisher={{ill|Aoki Shoten|ja|青木書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shoten|1=Aoki Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q44421}}|year=2009|pages=290-298|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Cluster Kofun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cluster Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609514}} * {{ill|Takayasu Senzuka Kofun|en|Takayasu Senzuka Kofun|qid=Q11669134|lt=Takayasu Kofun Cluster|1=Takayasu Senzuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|en|Ichisuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11353283|lt=Ichisuga Kofun Cluster|1=Ichisuka Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [https://kofunoheya.web.fc2.com/tofuken/oosaka/kasiwara/hirao/hirao2.htm Hirao Mountain Sen-tsuka Kofun Cluster] - Kofun Room * [http://kofunoheya.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-515.html Hirao Mountain Sen-tsuka Kofun Cluster Blog] - Kofun Room Blog {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|平尾山古墳群|version=100874943|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:平尾山古墳群]] {{Commons category|Hiraoyama Kofun Group}} {{wikidata link|Q11482454}} [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
